calendar.matters:
To Compel. Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Admissions
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Damon Thurston, Esq. 510-645-9437 Rankin Shuey Mintz Lampasona & Harper for Defendant.
No other Appearance

RULING - The matter is Discussed.

Party states appearance. The Court announces a notice to contest was received from the Plaintiff. This morning, the Court receives another email from the Plaintiff the contest is being withdrawn. At 9:15am, there is no appearance by the Plaintiff. The Court states the tentative ruling will be adopted. No Objection from Mr. Thurston. The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Tibor Szabo's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Admissions is OFF CALENDAR.

(see word minutes for full details)

Both parties ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Set For Trial On Jun-07-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Continued To May-06-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Tibor Tzabo 415-424-2981 Plaintiff in Pro Per. (In-person)
Damon Thurston, Esq. 510-645-9437 Rankin Shuey Mintz Lampasona & Harper for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received on all 3 motions.

ARGUED. Mr. Thurnston does not contests the court's TR and submitted a proposed order. Mr. Szabo contests the TR and presents argument. Mr. Szabo thanks the court for finding no legal basis for connecting the 2 lawsuits, rejecting exhibits of the Regents and will comply with the court's ruling and states he does not waive his default rights. (as stated on the other 2 motions.) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

1 - Defendant Regents of the University of California's demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion For Judcial Entry Of Default And Reconsideration Of Clerks Rejection
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Tibor Tzabo 415-424-2981 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Damon Thurston, Esq. 510-645-9437 Rankin Shuey Mintz Lampasona & Harper for Defendant. (In-person)

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received on all 3 motions.

ARGUED. Mr. Szabo contests the TR and presents argument. Mr. Szabo states "He is in pro per and he does not waive his objections of the default based on the fact that the Regents was extremely untimely and also failed to seek relief under 473. Having said that I will comply with the tentative ruling and request the minutes reflect I was here and that I did not waive my right to the Default.": The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

3 - Plaintiff Tibor Szabo's Motion For Judicial Entry Of Default And Reconsideration Of Clerks Rejection is DENIED.

The filing of a responsive pleading, even if late, prevents the entry of default. (Goddard v. Pollack (1974) 37 Cal.App.3d 137, 141.) Here, Defendant Regents of the University of California filed a demurrer, which is a responsive pleading, and the court has adjudicated the demurrer. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10.) On this record, the court declines to reconsider the clerk's rejection of Plaintiff's notices of default; the court, however, notes that the clerk's actions are well documented, and Plaintiff has not established they were inappropriate.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Tibor Tzabo 415-424-2981 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Damon Thurston, Esq. 510-645-9437 Rankin Shuey Mintz Lampasona & Harper for Defendant. (In-person)

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received on all 3 motions.

ARGUED. Mr. Szabo contests the TR and presents argument. Mr. Szabo states "He is in pro per and he does not waive his objections of the default based on the fact that the Regents was extremely untimely and also failed to seek relief under 473. Having said that, I will comply with the tentative ruling and request the minutes reflect I was here and that I did not waive my right to the Default.": The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

2 - Plaintiff Tibor Szabo's Motion to Strike Demurrer to Complaint is DENIED.

No good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion to strike Defendant Regents of the University of California's demurrer is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 473 is inapt. The court rejects as unsupported Plaintiff's contention Defendant filed its demurrer in bad faith.

Order is signed.
